summary: I was born in Somalia, but I had to flee the country on January 12, 1991 because of the Somali Civil War. Somalia is where my roots are, and even though I left, it has always been a significant part of my life. I returned to Kismayo in Somalia after two years, where my aunt lived and helped me learn business. Somalia represents both my past struggles and my beginnings.

After fleeing Somalia in 1991, I went to a small town called Doble, which is near the border of Kenya. It was a place of refuge for me during those difficult times. I stayed there for about two years before returning to Kismayo in Somalia. Doble was important as a safe stop during my escape from the civil war.

Kenya is the neighboring country near the town of Doble where I first fled after leaving Somalia in 1991. Although I did not settle in Kenya, its proximity to Doble made it a significant landmark during my journey. The border area between Somalia and Kenya was a critical passage for me as I sought safety from the civil war.

Kismayo is the city in Jubaland where I was born and where my aunt lived. After spending two years in Doble, I returned to Kismayo to reconnect with family and learn business from my aunt. It was in Kismayo that I started my own business one year after returning. This city holds a special place in my heart as both my birthplace and the start of my entrepreneurial journey.

Jubaland is the region where Kismayo is located, and it is where I was born and raised. Returning to Jubaland after fleeing Somalia allowed me to reconnect with my family and community. It was in this region that I learned business skills from my aunt and eventually started my own business. Jubaland represents my origins and the foundation of my work ethic.

Seven years after starting my business in Kismayo, I moved to South Africa to expand my business ventures. In South Africa, I dealt in clothing, shoes, and cosmetics. Although I worked hard there, I did not feel safe, which eventually led me to begin the resettlement process to the United States in 2001. South Africa was an important chapter in my life for business growth but lacked the security I needed.
cues: moved seven years after starting business

summary: I began my resettlement process to the United States on November 18, 2001, and finally arrived there on January 13, 2014. The United States has given me safety and freedom that I did not have in Somalia or South Africa. I am grateful to the US government and people for welcoming me and providing the documents and support I needed. Now, I am working and happy here, with hopes to open my own business someday and enjoy the freedom to work for myself.

The Somali Civil War forced me to flee my home country on January 12, 1991. This conflict disrupted my life and made it unsafe to remain in Somalia. The war was the reason I first went to Doble near Kenya and later moved through various places before resettling in the United States. The civil war shaped much of my journey and my search for safety and stability.
